title: Deploy a Node Monitoring DaemonSet
description:
Deploy a lightweight busybox monitoring agent as a DaemonSet and verify that exactly one Pod runs on every node.

**Focus**: Understanding the DaemonSet's core guarantee — one Pod per eligible node — and the difference from a Deployment with matching replica count.

## Common Pitfalls
- A DaemonSet has no `replicas` field — including one will cause a validation error.
- The Pod count will equal the number of schedulable nodes, not a fixed number you set.
- If your cluster has a control-plane taint and you don't add a toleration, the control-plane node is skipped silently.
steps: Create a DaemonSet named 'node-monitor' using image busybox:1.36 with command ['sh', '-c', 'while true; do echo "Monitoring node: $NODE_NAME"; sleep 30; done']
Add an env var NODE_NAME sourced from spec.nodeName using the downward API (fieldRef: spec.nodeName)
Apply the manifest and verify the DaemonSet is created: kubectl get daemonset node-monitor
Check the number of desired and ready pods matches the node count: kubectl get daemonset node-monitor
List pods with node placement: kubectl get pods -l app=node-monitor -o wide
Confirm each node has exactly one pod by checking the NODE column
hints: DaemonSets use apps/v1 apiVersion, same as Deployments
Do NOT add a replicas field — DaemonSets do not have one
To expose the node name inside the container, use env with valueFrom.fieldRef.fieldPath: spec.nodeName
kubectl get ds is shorthand for kubectl get daemonset
DESIRED in 'kubectl get ds' output shows how many nodes are eligible

editor_config.initial_code:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: node-monitor
  labels:
    app: node-monitor
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: node-monitor
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: node-monitor
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: monitor
        image: # TODO: Use busybox:1.36
        command: # TODO: ['sh', '-c', 'while true; do echo "Monitoring node: $NODE_NAME"; sleep 30; done']
        env:
        - name: NODE_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: # TODO: Which field exposes the node name to the pod?
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "10m"
            memory: "16Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: "50m"
            memory: "32Mi"

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: DaemonSet
metadata:
  name: node-monitor
  labels:
    app: node-monitor
spec:
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: node-monitor
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: node-monitor
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: monitor
        image: busybox:1.36
        command: ['sh', '-c', 'while true; do echo "Monitoring node: $NODE_NAME"; sleep 30; done']
        env:
        - name: NODE_NAME
          valueFrom:
            fieldRef:
              fieldPath: spec.nodeName
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "10m"
            memory: "16Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: "50m"
            memory: "32Mi"
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
set -e

kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

# Wait for DaemonSet to be ready
echo "Waiting for DaemonSet node-monitor..."
timeout=90
elapsed=0
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  desired=$(kubectl get ds node-monitor -o jsonpath='{.status.desiredNumberScheduled}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  ready=$(kubectl get ds node-monitor -o jsonpath='{.status.numberReady}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  if [ "$desired" -gt 0 ] && [ "$ready" = "$desired" ]; then break; fi
  sleep 5; ((elapsed+=5))
done

# Verify DaemonSet exists
desired=$(kubectl get ds node-monitor -o jsonpath='{.status.desiredNumberScheduled}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
ready=$(kubectl get ds node-monitor -o jsonpath='{.status.numberReady}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
if [ "$desired" -gt 0 ] && [ "$ready" = "$desired" ]; then
  echo "✓ DaemonSet node-monitor: $ready/$desired pods ready"
else
  echo "✗ DaemonSet not ready: $ready/$desired"
  kubectl describe ds node-monitor
  exit 1
fi

# Verify image
image=$(kubectl get ds node-monitor -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}')
if echo "$image" | grep -q "busybox"; then
  echo "✓ Using busybox image: $image"
else
  echo "✗ Unexpected image: $image"
  exit 1
fi

# Verify NODE_NAME env var is configured via downward API
fieldpath=$(kubectl get ds node-monitor -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].env[0].valueFrom.fieldRef.fieldPath}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ "$fieldpath" = "spec.nodeName" ]; then
  echo "✓ NODE_NAME env var uses downward API (spec.nodeName)"
else
  echo "✗ NODE_NAME fieldPath is '$fieldpath', expected spec.nodeName"
  exit 1
fi

# Verify each node has exactly one pod
node_count=$(kubectl get nodes --no-headers 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
pod_count=$(kubectl get pods -l app=node-monitor --no-headers 2>/dev/null | grep -c Running || true)
if [ "$pod_count" -le "$node_count" ] && [ "$pod_count" -gt 0 ]; then
  echo "✓ $pod_count pods running across $node_count nodes"
else
  echo "✗ Pod count ($pod_count) doesn't match expected node coverage"
  exit 1
fi

echo "✓ All validations passed"
exit 0
